samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>A simple Radix-2 Divider for 8-bit signed/unsigned numbers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">The clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal.</input>
        <input id="sign">Indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">The dividend input.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">The divisor input.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">Valid operation signal.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">Ready signal for the result.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">Result valid signal.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">The result output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="DIVIDE">1'b1</constant>
        <constant id="IDLE">1'b0</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <signal id="neg_divisor" width_description="[9:0]">The negative divisor.</signal>
        <signal id="sr" width_description="[9:0]">Shift register for storing the dividend.</signal>
        <signal id="sub_result" width_description="[9:0]">Subtraction result.</signal>
        <signal id="mux_out" width_description="[9:0]">Multiplexer output.</signal>
        <signal id="carry_out">Carry-out signal.</signal>
        <signal id="cnt">Counter for iterations.</signal>
        <signal id="start_cnt">Start counter signal.</signal>
        <signal id="abs_dividend" width_description="[7:0]">Absolute value of the dividend.</signal>
        <signal id="abs_divisor" width_description="[7:0]">Absolute value of the divisor.</signal>
        
        // Assigning values based on the sign input
        assign abs_dividend = (sign & dividend[7]) ? ~dividend + 1 : dividend;
        assign abs_divisor = (sign & divisor[7]) ? ~divisor + 1 : divisor;
        assign neg_divisor = {1'b0, ~abs_divisor} + 1;
        assign mux_out = carry_out ? sub_result : sr;
        assign sub_result = sr + neg_divisor;
        assign carry_out = sub_result[9];
        assign result = {sr[8:0], dividend[0]};
        assign res_valid = (~|cnt && !start_cnt) || (res_valid && res_ready);
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or reset
        always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                // Resetting all registers and signals
                cnt <= 0;
                start_cnt <= 0;
                sr <= 0;
                res_valid <= 0;
            end else begin
                // Handling the case where opn_valid is high and res_valid is low
                if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin
                    // Saving the dividend and divisor
                    sr <= {1'b0, abs_dividend};
                    // Initializing the shift register with the dividend
                    sr <= sr << 1;
                    // Setting the negative divisor
                    neg_divisor <= {1'b0, ~abs_divisor} + 1;
                    // Starting the division process
                    cnt <= 1;
                    start_cnt <= 1;
                    res_valid <= 0;
                end else if (!opn_valid && start_cnt) begin
                    // Incrementing the counter
                    cnt <= cnt + 1;
                    // Updating the shift register based on the subtraction result
                    sr <= mux_out;
                    // Clearing the start counter signal
                    start_cnt <= 0;
                    // Setting the result valid signal
                    res_valid <= 1;
                end else if (cnt == 8) begin
                    // Clearing the counter and start counter signals
                    cnt <= 0;
                    start_cnt <= 0;
                    // Setting the result valid signal
                    res_valid <= 1;
                end else if (res_valid && res_ready) begin
                    // Clearing the result valid signal
                    res_valid <= 0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
